patternDescription: Cost center blocked for primary postings
rootCauseTemplate: The cost center {costCenter} has an active posting block (lock indicator) for primary cost postings in fiscal year {fiscalYear} / period {fiscalPeriod}. This typically occurs after period-end closure or when finance controls restrict postings to specific cost centers.
recommendedAction: Unlock cost center via 'Manage Cost Centers' app or use an alternative active cost center. If unlock is not permitted, raise a workflow request to the Controlling team.

patternDescription: Cost center blocked for secondary postings
rootCauseTemplate: The cost center {costCenter} is locked for secondary cost postings (internal activity allocations, assessments, distributions) for the specified period. This prevents allocation cycles and internal orders from posting to this cost center.
recommendedAction: Check the lock indicators in cost center master data. Contact the CO team to unlock secondary posting if required for allocation runs.

patternDescription: Cost element not assigned to cost center category
rootCauseTemplate: The cost element {costElement} is not permitted for posting to cost center category {category}. The cost element category must match the allowed cost element groups defined for this cost center type.
recommendedAction: Reassign the cost element to a compatible cost center category, or post to a cost center that accepts this cost element type.

rootCauseTemplate: Fiscal period {fiscalPeriod}/{fiscalYear} is closed for account type {accountType} in company code {companyCode}. Period-end close has been completed and further postings are restricted.
recommendedAction: Request period opening from the Finance team via workflow if posting is business-critical. Alternatively, post in the next open period.

rootCauseTemplate: The available budget for cost center {costCenter} / cost element {costElement} in fiscal year {fiscalYear} has been exceeded by {excessAmount}. Budget tolerance threshold has been breached.
recommendedAction: Submit a budget transfer or supplement request via workflow. Use alternative cost center with available budget as interim measure.

rootCauseTemplate: User {userId} does not have the required authorization to perform action {action} on cost center {costCenter} in company code {companyCode}. Authorization object K_CSKS check failed.
recommendedAction: Contact your system administrator to review authorization object K_CSKS assignment. Raise an access request via the IT helpdesk if additional authorization is required.

patternDescription: Activity type not assigned to cost center
rootCauseTemplate: Activity type {activityType} is not assigned to cost center {costCenter} for fiscal year {fiscalYear}. Activity allocations and internal orders referencing this activity type cannot be processed.
recommendedAction: Assign the activity type to the cost center in period-specific activity type planning (KP26). Verify the activity type is active for the controlling area.

rootCauseTemplate: Cost center {costCenter} is not valid on posting date {postingDate}. The validity period defined in master data (from {validFrom} to {validTo}) does not cover the requested posting date.
recommendedAction: Extend the validity period of the cost center via 'Manage Cost Centers' app. Requires Controlling team authorization. Use an alternative valid cost center as interim.

rootCauseTemplate: Assessment/distribution cycle {cycleName} for cost center group {costCenterGroup} in period {period} could not be found or has not been defined. The allocation run cannot execute.
recommendedAction: Define the missing allocation cycle via 'Manage Allocations' app. Verify cycle name, sender/receiver definitions, and assessment cost element assignments.

rootCauseTemplate: Internal order {orderNumber} does not have a valid settlement rule for cost center {costCenter}. Settlement cannot be executed without a defined settlement receiver.
recommendedAction: Define a settlement rule for the internal order via OKO2/KO02 transaction. Assign cost center as settlement receiver with 100% distribution.

rootCauseTemplate: The company code derived from cost center {costCenter} ({derivedCC}) does not match the company code {enteredCC} entered in the document. Cross-company code postings require specific configuration.
recommendedAction: Verify the correct cost center for the company code being posted to. If cross-company posting is intended, ensure intercompany settings are configured.

rootCauseTemplate: Statistical key figure {skf} is not defined for cost center {costCenter} in period {period}/{fiscalYear}. Tracing factor-based allocations cannot be executed.
recommendedAction: Enter the statistical key figure values via KB31N transaction or the Statistical Key Figure Actual Data entry app. Rerun the allocation cycle after entry.

rootCauseTemplate: Cost element {costElement} is not assigned to any cost element group for controlling area {controllingArea}. This prevents proper cost categorization and reporting.
recommendedAction: Assign the cost element to the appropriate cost element group using KAH2 transaction. Coordinate with the Chart of Accounts team for correct grouping.

rootCauseTemplate: Planning version {planVersion} is not defined or not active for fiscal year {fiscalYear} in controlling area {controllingArea}. Cost center planning upload failed.
recommendedAction: Create or activate the planning version in 'Manage Plan Versions' app or via transaction OKEV. Verify version is open for input.

patternDescription: Goods receipt without purchase order reference
rootCauseTemplate: Goods receipt posting attempted without a valid purchase order reference for account assignment to cost center {costCenter}. GR/IR clearing account posting failed.
recommendedAction: Create or reference the correct purchase order with cost center account assignment. Verify the purchase order is open and has remaining quantity.

patternDescription: Profit center not assigned to cost center
rootCauseTemplate: Profit center is not assigned to cost center {costCenter} or the assigned profit center {profitCenter} is not valid on the posting date. Profit center derivation failed.
recommendedAction: Assign an active profit center to the cost center in cost center master data. Verify profit center validity period covers the posting date.

patternDescription: Budget not released for cost center
rootCauseTemplate: Budget for cost center {costCenter} has not been released for availability control in fiscal year {fiscalYear}. Commitment budget check cannot be performed.
recommendedAction: Release the budget via 'Manage Cost Center Budgets' app or KO22 transaction. Ensure budget is approved and released before enabling availability control.

rootCauseTemplate: Cost center {costCenter} is not assigned to any standard hierarchy node for controlling area {controllingArea}. Hierarchical reporting and allocation cycles referencing this hierarchy will fail.
recommendedAction: Assign the cost center to the appropriate standard hierarchy node in 'Manage Cost Center Groups' app or OKEON transaction.

patternDescription: Asset posting to inactive cost center
rootCauseTemplate: Asset {assetNumber} is assigned to cost center {costCenter} which is currently inactive or locked. Depreciation and asset postings cannot be processed.
recommendedAction: Reactivate the cost center or reassign the asset to an active cost center via asset master data change. Coordinate with Fixed Assets and Controlling teams.

patternDescription: Receiver cost center invalid for assessment
rootCauseTemplate: Receiver cost center {receiverCC} defined in assessment cycle {cycleName} is invalid, locked, or expired. Assessment posting to this receiver cannot be completed.
recommendedAction: Update the assessment cycle receiver definition with a valid active cost center. Alternatively, unlock or extend the receiver cost center validity.
